video:video.video:title: Russian strike damages port and energy infrastructure in Odesa
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian authorities said a drone attack overnight Thursday into Friday hit Odesa's port and energy infrastructure, damaging facilities and causing power disruptions.

video:video.video:title: Crowds revel at cherry blossoms in Tokyo as they near full bloom
video:video.video:description: Cherry blossoms blanket parks in Tokyo as they near full bloom, drawing large crowds of tourists and local residents to revel at the pastel pink flowers.

video:video.video:title: Towering fountain of lava seen in Hawaii’s Kilauea Volcano
video:video.video:description: A tower of lava was visible, shooting up inside the Kilauea volcano on the Big Island of Hawaii on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Russia strikes civilian infrastructure in Sumy region, local authorities report
video:video.video:description: Two people were injured in a Russian overnight drone attack on Sumy region between Thursday and Friday, Ukraine State Emergency Service reported.

video:video.video:title: Sheriff's deputy killed in central California while serving eviction notice
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a man shot and killed a central California sheriff’s deputy while authorities were serving an eviction notice.

video:video.video:title: Iran says talks are 'continuation of defence and a continuation of war'
video:video.video:description: Negotiations have begun between the United States and Iran in Pakistan, days after a fragile, two-week ceasefire was announced.

video:video.video:title: Africa gears up to welcome the pope
video:video.video:description: Four countries in Africa have begun preparations Friday to welcome Pope Leo XIV, who is set to visit Algeria, Angola, Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea.

video:video.video:title: Iraq elects Kurdish politician Nizar Amidi as president amid war fallout
video:video.video:description: Iraq’s parliament voted Saturday to elect Nizar Amidi, a political official with one of the country’s two main Kurdish parties, as president, five months after a parliamentary election that didn’t produce a bloc with a decisive majority.

video:video.video:title: Officials: Stabbings on New York subway leaves 3 hurt as officers shoot and kill knife-wielding man
video:video.video:description: A man with a machete who attacked three people randomly at a major New York City subway station on Saturday morning was shot and killed by police, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Peru deploys election materials, troops ahead of vote as long lines form for IDs
video:video.video:description: More than 300 trucks left the headquarters of Peru’s National Office of Electoral Processes early Saturday in southern Lima, carrying election materials for Sunday’s general election in Peru.

video:video.video:title: KATSEYE celebrate Coachella debut at NYLON afterparty
video:video.video:description: KATSEYE arrive at the NYLON House in the Desert party following their first Coachella performance. The group’s sixth member, Manon Bannerman, is currently on hiatus.
video:video.video:tag: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival

video:video.video:title: Tribal gas stations offer a reprieve from high prices during Iran war
video:video.video:description: Junelle Lewis was on the hunt for a reprieve from Seattle-area gas prices driven high by the Iran war when an app on her phone gave her the answer: the Tulalip Reservation north of the city, almost half an hour from her home.

video:video.video:title: Trump downplays Iran negotiations, says deal ‘makes no difference’
video:video.video:description: Speaking to reporters outside the White House on Saturday, U.S. President Donald Trump claimed military victory against Iran and downplayed the importance of ongoing ceasefire negotiations involving Vice President JD Vance because “regardless what happens we win.”

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 11th: US and Iranian officials meet in Pakistan in ceasefire talks; Russia continues strikes in Ukraine amid Orthodox Easter truce; Peru deploys supplies ahead of general election; Artemis II astronauts splashdown in the Pacific

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts emerge from their moon capsule after returning to Earth
video:video.video:description: The first astronauts to visit the moon in more than a half-century are back on Earth. Artemis II's crew of four have emerged one-by-one from their lunar capsule Friday after a splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: Elle Fanning and Michelle Pfeiffer star in new comedy 'Margo's Got Money Troubles'
video:video.video:description: "Margo's Got Money Troubles" stars Elle Fanning as a college student who struggles to make ends meet after having a baby.

video:video.video:title: Candles illuminate church of the Holy Sepulchre during Holy Fire ceremony
video:video.video:description: Candles illuminated the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem on Saturday as hundreds of Christian worshippers attended the Holy Fire ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Watch how Artemis II returned to Earth
video:video.video:description: Artemis II's crew of four have emerged one-by-one from their lunar capsule Friday after a splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: Funeral of 13 Lebanese service officers killed in Israeli strike on southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Thirteen members of Lebanon’s State Security Service were laid to rest on Saturday in Sidon, a day after being killed in an Israeli strike in the southern city of Nabatieh.

video:video.video:title: 350 Russian and Ukrainian POWs return home in prisoner exchange
video:video.video:description: 350 Russian and Ukrainian prisoners of war returned home on Saturday in the latest prisoner exchange between the two countries.

video:video.video:title: Attacks on Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Kramatorsk ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
video:video.video:description: At least two people were killed in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday as a result of a drone strike, local authorities reported, ahead of a proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed, 2 presumed dead after partial roof collapse at parking garage being built in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a stairwell roof at a parking garage being built in Philadelphia collapsed suddenly, killing one person and leaving two missing and presumed dead.

video:video.video:title: Bruno Mars, the 'New king of Las Vegas' is celebrated with Strip parade
video:video.video:description: Bruno Mars, the 'New king of Las Vegas' is celebrated with Strip parade

video:video.video:title: Artemis II returns home with Pacific splashdown
video:video.video:description: Artemis II’s astronauts returned from the moon with a dramatic splashdown in the Pacific on Friday to close out humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than a half-century.

video:video.video:title: Enter a giant world of play at new board games exhibition in Italy
video:video.video:description: What makes the perfect game and why do all humans like to play? How do games mirror society, relationships, and the way we understand reality?

video:video.video:title: Cheers erupt during Artemis II re-entry in San Diego watch party
video:video.video:description: Families and space fans attending a watch party at the San Diego Air & Space Museum cheer on as the first astronauts to visit the moon in more than a half-century splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: See thousands of decorated eggs representing Christ's resurrection ahead of Orthodox Easter
video:video.video:description: As Orthodox Easter approaches, Romania is keeping the tradition of decorated Easter eggs alive with two museums dedicated to the craft.

video:video.video:title: Sierra Leone’s women rickshaw drivers are redefining who belongs behind the wheel
video:video.video:description: On the bustling streets of Freetown, Sierra Leone, women are taking the handlebars and breaking into rickshaw driving, a job long dominated by men.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un meets Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi
video:video.video:description: Kim Jong Un voiced support for China’s push to build a “multipolar world” and called for deeper ties between the traditional allies during a meeting with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi, state media said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: US and Iranian delegations arrive in Pakistan for talks
video:video.video:description: The high-level talks between the US and Iranian representatives mark the first such meeting since the war began more than a month ago that will test whether they can shore up a fragile ceasefire and pave the way for peace.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza continue to face uncertainty six months after a fragile ceasefire took hold
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip continue to face uncertainty and fading hope six months after a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold.

video:video.video:title: Taraji P. Henson, Debbie Allen reflect on August Wilson’s legacy
video:video.video:description: Taraji P. Henson and Debbie Allen discuss the enduring relevance of August Wilson’s writing at a press event for the Broadway revival of “Joe Turner’s Come and Gone.”

video:video.video:title: Climate change is outpacing evolution. Scientists are using DNA to catch up
video:video.video:description: As climate change outpaces the ability of ecosystems to adapt, scientists are turning to conservation genomics to guide restoration. (AP Video: Annika Hammerschlag.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 10
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, April 10th: Vice President JD Vance to lead mediated talks with Iran; Netanyahu authorizes direct Israel-Lebanon negotiations; Melania Trump denies knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s crimes; Artemis II crew to splash down on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Vance warns Tehran not to 'play' the US as he heads to negotiations aimed at ending war with Iran
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance says he believes negotiations with Iran will be "positive." Boarding Air Force Two on his way to Pakistan, Vance added: “If they’re gonna try and play us, then they’re gonna find that the negotiating team is not that receptive.”

video:video.video:title: Gas price spike pushes up inflation by most in four years in March
video:video.video:description: The largest monthly jump in gas prices in six decades caused a sharp spike in inflation in March, heightening the political challenges for the White House.

video:video.video:title: World leaders react to 2-week ceasefire agreement between Iran and US
video:video.video:description: The world leaders welcomed the 2-week ceasefire agreement between Iran and US.

video:video.video:title: Two dead and 14 injured in fireworks warehouse explosion in Russia
video:video.video:description: An explosion at a fireworks warehouse in the southern Russian city of Vladikavkaz killed at least two people and injured 14 others, including a baby, local authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Rwanda tries to protect farmland in Africa’s most densely populated nation
video:video.video:description: Since September, Rwanda’s government has been mapping agricultural land and using satellite imagery to track any development encroaching on farmlands and forests in a country where the population is expected to reach 22 million in a couple of years.

video:video.video:title: Flyers fans have faith as run at playoff spot follows family's jersey gift to Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia Flyers fans took a unique approach to support their team by gifting Pope Leo XIV a customized Flyers jersey during a family trip to Italy.

video:video.video:title: Russia's internet crackdown leads to a spring of growing discontent
video:video.video:description: Moves toward internet control for years, Russia has sought to take the internet under total government control and potentially cut it off from the rest of the world.

video:video.video:title: Expectations for talks between Lebanon and Israel as Beirut mourns more than 300 dead
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese capital of Beirut was still reeling Friday from the shock of Israeli attacks two days prior, the deadliest day in the country since the renewed war between Israel and Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah erupted in March.

video:video.video:title: The faith-based AI boom raises hopes and concerns
video:video.video:description: The faith-based AI market is expanding, with tools for various religions. As AI tools designed with religion in mind become increasingly common, many are reckoning with how these technologies shape their relationship to faith and spiritual guidance.

video:video.video:title: AP photographers in Lebanon talk about their images on the deadliest day of the war
video:video.video:description: Photographers from The Associated Press in Lebanon talked about the impactful images they took on Wednesday, the deadliest day in the country since the renewed war between Israel and Iranian-backed militant group, Hezbollah that erupted on March.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the March consumer inflation index
video:video.video:description: The largest monthly jump in gas prices in six decades caused a sharp spike in inflation in March. It’s the first read on inflation to capture the effects of the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem believes music can unify
video:video.video:description: 2026 Eurovision contestant Delta Goodrem responds to the boycott of the song contest by five countries who did not want Israel to take part. (April 10)

video:video.video:title: Delta does Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Musician Delta Goodrem is winning over fans and gaining support for her bid to win the Eurovision Song Contest, where she'll be representing Australia with her song "Eclipse."

video:video.video:title: AP photographers in Lebanon talk about their images on the deadliest day of the war
video:video.video:description: Photographers from The Associated Press in Lebanon talked about the impactful images they took on Wednesday, the deadliest day in the country since the renewed war between Israel and Iranian-backed militant group, Hezbollah that erupted on March.

video:video.video:title: 'I'm thinking about it': Kamala Harris says she's considering a 2028 White House bid
video:video.video:description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris says she’s considering another presidential bid. She made the comments Friday during the National Action Network’s annual convention, where more than a half-dozen Democratic 2028 presidential prospects appeared.

video:video.video:title: In his first 100 days, Mamdani brings a unique star power to New York City governance
video:video.video:description: In his first 100 days in office, New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has governed with a star power unusual in politics. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: How Pakistan has emerged as a mediator between US and Iran
video:video.video:description: It was initially seen as an unexpected mediator, but this week Pakistan has established itself as a key player in bringing Iran and the United States to the negotiating table.

video:video.video:title: High-powered delegation from Iran arrives in Pakistan for U.S. peace talks
video:video.video:description: A high-powered delegation from Iran, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf accompanied by Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, arrived in Islamabad late Friday to participate in peace talks with the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 10th: Vice President JD Vance warned Iran not to “play” the U.S. before mediated talks; Hezbollah supporters in Beirut protest Lebanon-Israel scheduled talks; Five people have been charged with murder in fireworks warehouse explosion; Suspect arrested for throwing

video:video.video:title: Without fuel, Cubans use their well-known creativity to get around the city
video:video.video:description: As the fuel crisis in Cuba continues, the island's residents are switching up how they travel. (AP shot by Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán

video:video.video:title: Vance heads overseas for negotiations aimed at ending Iran war
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance is warning Iran not to “play” the U.S. as he heads for negotiations aimed at ending the war.

video:video.video:title: Ahead of talks, Trump confident the Strait of Hormuz will open 'with or without' Iran's cooperation
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump voiced confidence Friday ahead of Vice President JD Vance’s high-stakes talks in Pakistan with Iranian officials, calling Tehran “militarily defeated” and vowing the Strait of Hormuz will reopen “with or without” them.

video:video.video:title: Iranian-American says his family in Tehran wants the Iranian regime gone
video:video.video:description: Iranian-American Ali Khaleghi talked to his family in Tehran two weeks ago and said they were anxious, not because of the war going on in their country, but because the Iranian regime is still there.

video:video.video:title: People react after Israeli Prime Minister approved direct talks with Lebanon
video:video.video:description: People react after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday that he has authorized direct negotiations with Lebanon aimed at disarming Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants and establishing relations between the two countries.

video:video.video:title: Sweat, plunge, repeat: UK embraces the sauna boom
video:video.video:description: Scandinavians have long-embraced the ritual of the sauna, but now people in the UK are signing up for the hot experience - with a floating pod on the River Thames.

video:video.video:title: DHS briefing on arrest of Haitian immigrant charged in brutal Florida killing
video:video.video:description: Rolbert Joachin, 40, was arrested and charged with killing a woman on April 2 in Fort Myers, about 160 miles northwest of Miami.

video:video.video:title: Gaudí confirmed as architect of unique mountain refuge
video:video.video:description: A long-running debate over a mountain chalet in Catalonia may finally have been settled. Officials now say the unusual building was designed by none other than Antoni Gaudí.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II’s astronauts return to Earth with a splashdown in the Pacific
video:video.video:description: The three Americans and one Canadian have returned with a dramatic splashdown Friday evening, as their capsule parachuted into the Pacific to close out a nearly 10-day trip to the moon and back.

video:video.video:title: Top moments from Artemis II
video:video.video:description: Check out some of Artemis II's best moments from NASA's historic lunar comeback!

video:video.video:title: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts return home to cheers after a record-breaking trip
video:video.video:description: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts are back home and feted to a thunderous welcome. Still marveling over their record-breaking lunar fly-around, the crew of four flew to Houston's Ellington Field from San Diego on Saturday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV blasts ‘delusion of omnipotence’ fuelling US-Israeli war in Iran
video:video.video:description: In his strongest words yet, Pope Leo XIV on Saturday denounced the “delusion of omnipotence” that is fuelling the U.S.

video:video.video:title: US and Iranian delegations meet Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif in Islamabad
video:video.video:description: The ceasefire brokered by Pakistan still faces hurdles in the talks beginning Saturday, as Israel and Hezbollah militants have been trading fire along the border of southern Lebanon and Iran has set conditions before negotiations can begin.

video:video.video:title: Haitians cut back on already scarce food and ask how they'll survive rising fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Haiti, the most impoverished country in the Western Hemisphere, has been hit the hardest by rising oil prices that experts warn will deepen a spiraling humanitarian crisis.

video:video.video:title: US and Iran end ceasefire talks and Vance heads home without an agreement
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement at the end of historic, face-to-face talks after the U.S. said Tehran refused to commit to not developing a nuclear weapon, leaving uncertainty over a fragile, two-week ceasefire.
